template.name: SplitVoteCounter
Node

location.name: StableLeader
location.label: stableTimer' == 0

The machine moves to this state once it has sent enough heartbeats to safely say it is a stable leader.
This is to make the state space finite, which allows the model checker to verify it.

transition.label: timer == timeout && votes[id] < ((N/2)+1)
currentTerm:=(++currentTerm), votes[id]:=1, voted:=true, timer:=0, timeout := rand(ET_MIN, ET_MAX), sendVoteRequest()
A new timeout is randomly selected each time the the Candidate starts a new term to prevent repeated split votes.
heartbeat?
timer:=0, currentTerm:= max(currentTerm, heartbeatTerm), voted:=false

Follower's election timeout resets when it receives a heartbeat from Leader.
Also, voted is set to false in prep for the next term.
votes[id]>=((N/2)+1) && timer <= timeout
heartbeatTerm=max(heartbeatTerm, currentTerm), timeout := rand(HBT_MIN, HBT_MAX), votes[id]:=0, voted:=false, timer:=0, heartbeatCounter:=0

template.name: Network

location.label: Setting the location's rate of exponential to λ=100 means Pr[leaving location after t]= 1−exp(-100t). This means the statistical model checker will take the transition basically as soon as it's enabled.

query.formula: E[<=10000; 20000] (max: node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader + node5.StableLeader + node6.StableLeader)
query.comment: Estimate the maximum number of allowed stable leaders
Estimate the maximum time it takes to reach a stable leader.

plot.title: Probability Density Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=36.796, bucket count=100 Runs: 10000 in total, 10000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4693.56, 8373.2] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 6564.91 ± 16.0001 (95% CI)

plot.title: Probability Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=36.796, bucket count=100 Runs: 10000 in total, 10000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4693.56, 8373.2] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 6564.91 ± 16.0001 (95% CI)

plot.title: Cumulative Probability Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=36.796, bucket count=100 Runs: 10000 in total, 10000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4693.56, 8373.2] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 6564.91 ± 16.0001 (95% CI)

plot.title: Cumulative Probability Confidence Intervals
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=36.796, bucket count=100 Runs: 10000 in total, 10000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4693.56, 8373.2] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 6564.91 ± 16.0001 (95% CI)

plot.title: Frequency Histogram
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=36.796, bucket count=100 Runs: 10000 in total, 10000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4693.56, 8373.2] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 6564.91 ± 16.0001 (95% CI)

query.formula: Pr[<=7220](<> node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader + node5.StableLeader + node6.StableLeader == 1)
query.comment: Estimate the probability of reaching a stable state in less than 3.5 seconds

plot.title: Probability Density Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.001, ε=0.05, bucket width=230.57, bucket count=13 Runs: 149 in total, 149 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4136, 7133] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 5652 ± 207.8 (99.9% CI)

plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.001, ε=0.05, bucket width=230.57, bucket count=13 Runs: 149 in total, 149 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4136, 7133] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 5652 ± 207.8 (99.9% CI)

query.formula: Pr[<=15000](<> node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader + node5.StableLeader + node6.StableLeader == 1)

plot.title: Probability Density Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=464.78, bucket count=9 Runs: 72 in total, 72 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [4289, 8472] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 5964 ± 203.5 (95% CI)
